Matchmaking Simulations

This section describes how to test your title by using MatchSim as a matchmaking server. The discussion is presented in the following topics.

  • Saving and Restoring Matchmaking Simulator Files
  • Running Matchmaking Simulator as a Server

Saving and Restoring Matchmaking Simulator Files

After modifying schema and query information to your liking, you can save a query definition file by clicking Save on the toolbar (it is marked with a disk icon), by clicking Save on the File menu, or by pressing Ctrl+S. The Matchmaking Simulator saves its query definitions as a file with an .xms extension. The .xms file you produce with MatchSim is what Microsoft uses to create your matchmaking queries on the matchmaking server.

To restore a previously saved query definition file, click Open on the tool bar (it is marked with an open folder icon), click Open on the File menu, or press Ctrl+O.

The MatchSim tool can edit multiple query definition files simultaneously. To create a new definition from scratch, click New on the tool bar (it is marked with an empty page icon), click New on the File menu, or press Ctrl+N.

Running Matchmaking Simulator as a Server

The MatchSim tool can function as a local matchmaking server for testing your game title's matchmaking code. You must first set up your Xbox Development Kit so it can communicate with Matchmaking Simulator. To do so, on the Tools menu, click Connect Xbox. This command causes MatchSim to add the following line to the xonline.ini file on your Xbox Development Kit:

S6:1.2.3.4:8080

The 1.2.3.4 portion is the IP address of the development system that is running MatchSim. With this line present in the xonline.ini file, all Matchmaking calls from the Xbox Development Kit are redirected to MatchSim (instead of being sent to the regular Xbox Live servers). To remove the S6 line from your development kit's xonline.ini file and restore regular Matchmaking operation, click Disconnect Xbox on the MatchSim Tools menu.

To start the Matchmaking Simulator tool server mode, click Start Server on the tool bar (it has a triangular "play" icon), or click Start Server on the Match menu. To stop the server, click Stop Server (it has a square "stop" icon), or click Stop Server on the Match menu.

Note    You must use the unsecure version of XONLINE—XONLINED.LIB or XONLINE.LIB—to test against the Matchmaking Simulator. When using these libraries, title-to-title communication can still be secure, depending on the use of parameters passed to XNetStartup. Communication with the Xbox Live datacenter is always securely encrypted. Other than the redirection of all Matchmaking traffic to the Matchmaking Simulator server, communication with the Xbox Live datacenter will not be affected by the use of Matchmaking Simulator as a Server.
